summary: ISS-1191 (Part 8b 8b.5/8b.7 self-referential commitment language overstatement) resolved by EXP-399: two single-sentence patches inside SEC-8b 8b.7 closing-commitment prose. PATCH-399-A softens 'doing more work than a review that cites only the latest version' (self-comparison framing) to 'higher bar than only current wording, because it ties accountability to the dated record; whether our prose consistently meets that bar is an empirical question, not a self-attestation.' PATCH-399-B replaces 'already in force across this review; this section makes it explicit' (totalizing self-claim flagged by curmudgeon as unverified) with explicit temporal scoping ('in force for citations and predictions registered from 2026-04-18 forward') and a tracked legacy-audit follow-up. Curmudgeon SEC-8b.c1 Hole 2 Recommendation A (soften-and-file). Implements the soften half; requests the decider to file the proposed_follow_up_issue (legacy citation-compliance audit, minor) and substitute its ISS-NNNN into PATCH-399-B's proposed_text in place of 'a follow-up open issue.' for a concrete cross-reference. Test impact: none expected (string-content edits on prose, no numerical/structural change).
decider_actions_requested: Apply PATCH-399-A and PATCH-399-B to data/sections.json part8b.html
File ISS-NNNN per proposed_follow_up_issue block (legacy citation-compliance audit)
Substitute the new ISS-NNNN ID into PATCH-399-B's proposed_text — replace 'tracked as a follow-up open issue.' with 'tracked as ISS-NNNN.'
Mark ISS-1191 status=fixed-pending-verification with fix_details referencing EXP-399
Rebuild HTML (node build.js html) and run tests/run.sh
